attempt.claim: Erdos #124 (open BEGL second question): reduces the open conjecture to a single interval-block lemma. Proven (GPT-Pro proposed, Opus-verified): (A) the 0-1 subset-sum set Sigma(S(D,k)) of all powers d^i (d in D, i>=k) has bounded gaps when sum 1/(d-1) >= 1; (B) gcd(D)=1 yields a complete residue system mod every M; (C) the sorted-power surplus T_n - a_{n+1} -> infinity, via Baker/Shorey-Tijdeman linear-forms-in-logarithms; plus the elementary interval-block criterion: if S = B disjoint-union F with Sigma(B) L-syndetic and Sigma(F) containing L consecutive integers, then Sigma(S) is cofinite. The remaining open core is existence of such an interval-block partition for every admissible finite D,k -- NOT implied by bounded-gaps + residues + surplus alone (the sequence 3,4,8,16,... has gcd 1 and bounded gaps yet misses a positive proportion mod 4). Computationally verified on D={3,4,7},k=1: largest non-representable integer = 581 (matches BEGL), cofinite past it, max gap 3. This maps the problem to its open core; it is not a proof of the conjecture.
attempt.detail: {"computational_check":"scripts/verify_124_begl_framework.py: {3,4,7} k=1 largest non-rep=581, cofinite past, max gap 3","k0_foothold":"first question (k=0) solved & Lean-formalized by Alexeev via Aristotle; this reduction shows it lifts only to bounded gaps, not cofiniteness","lean_followup":"the interval-block criterion implication is elementary and Lean-formalizable (turns the open target into the interval-block lemma)","melfi":"Melfi's small-reciprocal completeness is infinite-D, consistent with finite-D necessity (not Crossref-indexed; cited in BEGL/erdosproblems)","missing_core":"existence of an interval-block partition for every admissible finite D,k (the BEGL Szemeredi/Prouhet route needs long APs of bases, absent for finite D; {3,4,7} done by explicit computation + Mignotte-Waldschmidt bounds)","open_target_lean":"erdos124.ne_zero (formal-conjectures/ErdosProblems/124.lean): forall k!=0, D with d>=3, sum 1/(d-1)>=1, gcd=1 => all large n in sum_{d in D} sumsOfDistinctPowers d k","pillars_proven":["Prop A bounded gaps: a_{n+1} <= T_n + ceil(C_k(D)), C_k(D)=sum d^k/(d-1); interval-union induction","Prop B complete residues mod every M (gcd=1), generalizing BEGL Claim 4","Prop C surplus T_n - a_{n+1} -> infinity, reduces to a^m - b^n = c finiteness (Baker; Shorey-Tijdeman 1986; Mignotte 1989)","interval-block criterion (elementary, Lean-ready): Sigma(B) L-syndetic + Sigma(F) has L consecutive => Sigma(S) cofinite"],"provenance_note":"GPT-Pro produced the reduction + three pillars; Opus verified the 581 computation, the {3,4,8,16} obstruction, and the Diophantine citations (Crossref)."}

provenance.proposer: GPT-Pro (reduction + 3 pillars) + Opus 4.8 (581 computation + obstruction check + citation verification + bank)
